lessons.t: A Query Returns a Table; NULL = Unknown
Define the Metric Before You Compute It
Running Totals with a Window Frame
Build the P&L from the Ledger

gloss.d: A function that computes a single result from multiple rows — e.g., COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN.
A temporary name assigned to a column or table using AS. Makes queries more readable.
A value that is either TRUE or FALSE. WHERE conditions evaluate to booleans.
SQL's conditional logic. Evaluates conditions in order and returns the first matching result.
A named, temporary result set defined with WITH. Improves readability for complex queries.
A keyword-led section of a SQL statement, e.g., SELECT, FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY.
Returns the first non-NULL value from a list of arguments.
A named attribute in a table. Each column has a data type.
A rule enforced on table data, e.g., NOT NULL, UNIQUE, PRIMARY KEY.
Defines what kind of value a column holds: TEXT, INTEGER, DECIMAL, DATE, etc.
Keyword that removes duplicate rows from query results.
A column that references the primary key of another table, linking the two.
Clause that divides rows into groups so aggregate functions can summarize each group.
Filters groups after GROUP BY. Use it where you'd use WHERE but on aggregate results.
A database structure that speeds up lookups on a column — like a book's index.
Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
The absence of a value. Not zero, not empty string — unknown. Test with IS NULL.
A column (or set of columns) that uniquely identifies each row in a table.
A SQL statement that retrieves data. Starts with SELECT.
The process of comparing datasets to find gaps, duplicates, and mismatches.
A single record in a table.
The structure of a database: tables, columns, data types, and relationships.
A SELECT statement nested inside another SQL statement.
A function that computes a value across a set of rows (a window) without collapsing them.
Keeps all rows from the right table; fills NULLs for unmatched left-table columns.
Returns all rows from both tables; simulated in MySQL via UNION of LEFT and RIGHT JOINs.
SQL tools for working with dates: CURDATE(), DATE_SUB(), DATE_FORMAT(), DATEDIFF().
A B-Tree structure built on a column to speed up reads. Best on high-cardinality WHERE/JOIN columns.
Number of distinct values in a column. High cardinality (e.g. email) benefits from indexing; low cardinality (e.g. gender) does not.
A session-scoped physical table. Stores intermediate data; deleted when the connection closes.
A stored SQL query that acts like a virtual table. No physical data — re-runs on every SELECT.

lessons.t: How a Spreadsheet Is Built (the Grid)
Define the Metric Before You Compute It
Running Totals with an Expanding Range
Time Value of Money: NPV & IRR

gloss.d: A value or reference passed into a function. =SUM(B2:B10) has one argument: B2:B10.
A formula that operates on multiple values at once. Modern Excel handles many array operations automatically.
The address of a cell (e.g. A2, B10). Can be relative (changes when copied) or absolute ($A$2, stays fixed).
A reference that doesn't change when copied. Use $ signs: $A$1 locks both row and column.
=COUNTBLANK(range) — counts empty cells. Use it to audit data completeness before reporting.
How Excel stores a value: number, text, date, boolean, or error. Mismatched types cause silent failures.
Double-clicking a PivotTable value to see the underlying rows.
A formula that spills results into multiple cells automatically (Excel 365+). =UNIQUE() and =FILTER().
Excel's way of flagging a broken formula: #N/A, #VALUE!, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #NAME?, #NULL!, #NUM!.
In VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, means the lookup value must match precisely (FALSE or 0). Use this almost always.
The Filters zone narrows the entire PivotTable to rows matching a criterion.
The bar above the spreadsheet showing the actual formula, not just the result.
The totals row/column at the bottom or right of a PivotTable.
The first row containing column names. Required for PivotTables to work.
=ISNUMBER(A2) returns TRUE if the cell contains a number. Your go-to debugging tool when SUM returns 0.
A label you assign to a cell or range (e.g. 'TaxRate'). Makes formulas like =B2*TaxRate readable.
A special space character (CHAR(160)) that TRIM() can't remove. Fix with =SUBSTITUTE(A2,CHAR(160)," ").
The internal snapshot of your data that a PivotTable works from. If new rows don't appear, refresh it.
Right-click → Refresh pulls new data into the pivot.
An interactive filter button for PivotTables. Click to filter, Ctrl+click for multiple values.
Formula syntax that references a Table by name: =Table1[Revenue]. Auto-expands with new rows.
A named, structured range created with Ctrl+T. Auto-expands, works with structured references.
Data → Text to Columns splits one column into multiple, or converts text-numbers to real numbers.
=TRIM(text) removes leading, trailing, and extra internal spaces. First fix when lookups fail.
* matches any characters, ? matches one character. Used in COUNTIF, SUMIF, VLOOKUP.
Excel's built-in ETL tool (Data → Get Data). Records data-cleaning steps once and replays them on Refresh.
The multi-cell output of a dynamic array formula like FILTER or UNIQUE. Reference with the # operator.
Review → Protect Sheet locks all Locked cells so they can't be edited.
An average where each value contributes proportionally to its volume. =SUMPRODUCT(price,qty)/SUM(qty).

lessons.t: How Power BI Sees Data (Table & Grain)
Data Types & Why They Matter in Power BI
Load vs Shape vs Analyze (The Power BI Loop)
Define the Measure Before You Compute It
Time Value of Money: NPV in DAX

gloss.d: Combining multiple values into one — SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN.
A saved state of a report page — used for navigation, guided stories, or toggle effects.
A column added to a table using DAX, computed row by row when data is refreshed.
A table created entirely from a DAX expression rather than from a data source.
The uniqueness of values in a relationship column. One-to-many is the standard for star schemas.
Controls which direction a relationship propagates filters — single (one way) or both.
Data Analysis Expressions — the formula language for measures and calculated columns in Power BI.
A dedicated dimension table covering every date in your data range, required for time intelligence.
A lookup table (customers, products, regions) containing descriptive attributes used for grouping and filtering.
A navigation technique allowing users to right-click a data point and jump to a detail page.
The central table containing measurable, numeric business events — sales rows, clicks, transactions.
The combination of all active filters at the moment a measure is evaluated.
Software that connects the Power BI Service to on-premise data sources for scheduled refresh.
A dynamic DAX calculation that recalculates based on the current filter context.
The ETL engine inside Power BI for connecting to, transforming, and loading data.
The action of sending a .pbix report from Power BI Desktop to the Power BI Service.
The evaluation context in calculated columns — DAX processes one row at a time.
On-canvas visuals that act as interactive filters for end users.
A modeling pattern with one fact table surrounded by related dimension tables.
A collaboration container in the Power BI Service for managing reports, datasets, and access.
The DAX function for evaluating expressions under a modified filter context — the foundation of advanced DAX.
A set of DAX functions for period-based calculations — YTD, prior year, rolling averages.

lessons.t: Field Types & Why Tableau Colors Them
From One Sheet to a Workbook

gloss.d: How Tableau collapses multiple row values into a single number for display. Default is SUM.
A new field you define using a formula. Lives in the Data pane and works like any real field.
Controls the X-axis or column headers. Discrete fields create separate columns; continuous fields create a single axis.
A filter that runs before all other filters. Required when using Top N filters with dimension filters.
Count Distinct. Returns the number of unique values in a field. Different from COUNT, which counts all rows.
A canvas containing multiple views, text, and images. Views are live and can interact via Dashboard Actions.
Interactivity between views on a dashboard. Types: Filter, Highlight, URL, Go to Sheet.
A Marks card shelf that disaggregates the view without adding color or size.
A categorical field — Region, Product, Rep. Splits data into groups. Shown in blue in the Data pane.
Showing row-level values instead of aggregated ones. Uncheck Analysis → Aggregate Measures.
A field type that creates separate, distinct headers or buckets. Shown as a blue pill.
A field type that creates a single axis with a range of values. Shown as a green pill.
A snapshot of data saved in Tableau's .hyper format. Faster than Live connections for large data.
Advanced calculated field syntax that controls aggregation scope independent of the view. FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE.
Queries the data source in real time — always current. Slower than Extract for large datasets.
Controls how data is visually encoded: Color, Size, Label, Detail, Tooltip, Shape.
A numeric field that gets aggregated — Revenue, Units, Profit. Shown in green in the Data pane.
A user-controlled input variable in Tableau. Used in calculated fields for interactive filters or thresholds.
A horizontal or vertical line drawn at a specific value. Added via the Analytics pane.
Controls the Y-axis or row headers. SUM(Revenue) on Rows sets bar heights.
A named subset of dimension members. Condition-based, top N, or manual.
The chart-type suggester in the top-right corner of Tableau Desktop.
A presentation format in Tableau — a sequence of views with narrative captions (Story Points).
Text shown when hovering over a mark. Customized via Worksheet → Tooltip.
A statistical line through scatter or line chart data points. Added via the Analytics pane.

lessons.t: What a Library Is & Why pandas (import)
What a DataFrame Really Is (Rows, Cols, Index, Grain)
Define the Metric Before You Compute It
Build the P&L from a Ledger
Time Value of Money: NPV & IRR

gloss.d: The core pandas data structure — a 2D table with labeled rows and columns.
A one-dimensional labeled array — a single column from a DataFrame. df['revenue'] returns a Series.
Data type of a column: int64, float64, object (usually strings), bool, datetime64.
pandas label for columns with strings or mixed types. Numbers stored as text come in as object.
Not a Number — pandas representation of a missing value. Use isnull() to detect it, never == NaN.
Filtering rows by passing a True/False Series as a mask: df[df['revenue'] > 100].
The split-apply-combine pattern. groupby() splits data into groups; an aggregation applies a function.
Combine two DataFrames on a shared key. Equivalent to SQL JOIN. Types: inner (default), left, right, outer.
A parameter on many pandas methods. inplace=True modifies the DataFrame directly.
Moves the current index into a regular column. Commonly needed after groupby().
Calling multiple methods in sequence: df.groupby('region')['revenue'].sum().reset_index().
An operation applied to every element in a Series or DataFrame at once — without a Python loop.
Enables string methods on an entire Series: df['col'].str.lower().
In dropna() and drop_duplicates(), limits the operation to specified columns.
Convert a column's data type: df['units'].astype(int). Raises ValueError if a value can't convert.
Safely convert a column to numeric. errors='coerce' turns unconvertible values into NaN.
Replace NaN values with a specified or computed value. Returns a new Series/DataFrame — assign it back.
Remove rows (or columns) with NaN. Use subset= to only drop rows where a specific column is null.
Count occurrences of each unique value in a Series. Sorted by frequency.
Filter rows where a column's value is in a given list: df[df['region'].isin(['West','East'])].
A completely independent clone at every level. In pandas: df.copy() (deep=True by default).
A new container that shares the underlying inner data. In pandas: df.copy(deep=False).
NOT a copy — both names point to the same DataFrame object. Always use df.copy().
A function that wraps another function to add behavior. @my_decorator above a def.
Decorator marking a method that doesn't need self — a utility function on a class.
Decorator that lets you call a method like an attribute — obj.value instead of obj.value().
Decorator that memoizes a function's return values for repeated calls.
Return the n rows with the highest or lowest values in a column.
A port of CPython to WebAssembly, allowing Python to run in the browser.
The primary Python library for data analysis. Provides DataFrame and Series.
Row labels in a DataFrame or Series. After groupby, the group key becomes the index.
0 = rows (default), 1 = columns. df.drop('revenue', axis=1) drops a column.

lessons.t: Describe vs Infer (the Map of the Whole Kit)

gloss.d: Arithmetic average: sum of values divided by count. Sensitive to outliers.
Middle value in sorted data. Resistant to outliers and skew.
Most frequently occurring value. Can be used with categorical data.
Average of squared deviations from the mean. Units are original² (e.g., $²).
Square root of variance. Same units as the data. Most common spread measure.
Interquartile range = Q3 − Q1. Measures spread of the middle 50%.
The value below which P% of observations fall. Median = 50th percentile.
(value − mean) / std dev. Standardizes any value to standard deviation units from the mean.
Symmetric bell curve characterized by mean and std dev.
Measure of distribution asymmetry. Positive = right tail, Negative = left tail.
A value far from the bulk of the data. Commonly beyond 1.5×IQR from Q1/Q3.
A value 0–1 quantifying the likelihood of an event occurring.
The entire group you want to draw conclusions about.
A subset of the population used to estimate population characteristics.
Systematic over- or under-representation of certain groups in a sample.
Statistical measure of how strongly two variables move together (r from −1 to +1).
A direct mechanism by which one variable changes another. Requires experimental evidence.
A third variable influencing both measured variables, creating spurious correlation.
The default 'no effect' assumption in hypothesis testing.
Probability of observing the data (or more extreme) if H₀ is true. Low p = evidence against H₀.
p < α — the result is unlikely under H₀. Does not imply practical importance.
Randomized experiment comparing control and treatment groups on a single metric.
A range of values likely to contain the true population parameter at a stated confidence level.
Std dev / √n — measures precision of the sample mean as an estimate of the population mean.
Falsely rejecting H₀ (false positive). Probability is controlled by α.
